generated_from: agent registry and CPU/GPU placement prototype requirements
metadata.candidate_identity_policy: Candidate names are role labels for prototype policy wiring, not verified provider benchmark claims.
metadata.actual_model_policy: The model field is an internal role label. actual_model is a placeholder or a future linked LLM model name.
metadata.benchmark_status_policy: If benchmark_status is not_executed, selected_actual_model must not be claimed as a final benchmark-selected model.
notes: Current values are manually defined prototype policy baselines for LLM selection wiring.
Current scores are not actual LLM API benchmark results.
Actual LLM evaluation requires API keys from environment variables and a separate evaluation run.
These values are not final standardized benchmark results.
For a final quantitative report, regenerate scores from standardized per-model Ops evaluation runs.
The submission/demo package does not depend on external benchmark runners.

quality_first.description: Ops detection, safe action validity, and consistent reasoning are prioritized for the main research agent path.

cost_first.description: Cost and latency are prioritized for inexpensive repeated smoke tests and non-critical prechecks.

candidates.role: primary Ops reasoning model for service-control decisions
notes: Use as the default prototype policy candidate for 4-Agent Ops reasoning.
Useful for repeated non-critical test runs where low cost matters.
Use as the second coding/LLM agent for cross-validation of generated code, tests, and documentation.
Complements the primary reasoning model rather than replacing it.
